evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG requester through tck_i/tms_i/td_i
evidence_slots.operation: issue read/write DMI transactions and trigger debug actions
evidence_slots.object: debug module DMI request path and debug capabilities: debug_req/debug_resp to dm_top, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMI requests reaching debug functionality should be gated by persistent successful authentication/unlock state such as jtag_unlock/pass_check/hashValid, not only TAP DMIACCESS/update_dr selection.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag_tap can select DMIACCESS by IR value; dmi_jtag captures address/data on dmi_access && update_dr and permits READ transition when (pass_check | ~we_flag == 1). pass_check is defaulted low in combinational logic and jtag_unlock_o is assigned to pass_check, so it is not shown as a persistent unlock state. The request valid is forwarded through dmi_cdc to riscv_peripherals debug_req_valid/debug_req and dm_top.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG pins -> riscv_peripherals.i_dmi_jtag -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS/update_dr -> dmi_jtag Read/Write -> dmi_cdc -> riscv_peripherals.debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top DMI input
evidence_slots.state_condition: On trst_ni reset, dmi_jtag clears dr_q/state_q/address_q/data_q/error_q. In Idle, pass_check is defaulted to 0; READ is allowed if ~we_flag is 1, even with no pass_check. dmi_reset only clears DMI error state; dmi_rst_no is assigned rst_ni and top leaves it unconnected.
evidence_slots.impact: A locked JTAG requester may issue DMI reads when we_flag_4 is deasserted, reaching debug-module DMI logic without successful authentication, potentially exposing or influencing debug state depending on dm_top semantics.

rtl_evidence.detail: jtag_unlock_o assigned pass_check; dmi_req_valid/pass_check/pass_mode default low; Idle READ allowed by (pass_check | ~we_flag == 1) while WRITE requires pass_check; Read/Write assert dmi_req_valid; dmi_cdc connects local valid/request to outputs.
request CDC forwards jtag_dmi_valid_i/data to core_dmi_valid_o/core_dmi_req_o.
i_dmi_jtag connects dmi_req_o to debug_req and dmi_req_valid_o to debug_req_valid; dm_top receives debug_req_valid/debug_req.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: DMI read requests can reach the debug module without a persistent successful JTAG/HMAC unlock when we_flag is deasserted; only writes appear to require the transient pass_check condition.
inspection_records.notes: Based on provided model observation for lines not fully visible in raw snippets plus raw top/CDC snippets.

evidence_slots.subject: software/MMIO/NoC requester reaching fuse_mem through pkt_wrapper fuse_req/fuse_addr
evidence_slots.operation: read/expose authentication hash or fuse words
evidence_slots.object: jtag_hash / authentication fuse memory contents and other fuse-resident key material
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Fuse/hash contents used for debug authentication should be non-readable or readable only through explicit privilege/access-control/lock mediation with sensitive ranges filtered.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: fuse_mem stores the JTAG expected HMAC hash in the same constant mem array used for rdata_o. On req_i, addr_q captures addr_i; rdata_o returns mem[addr_q] when in range. No privilege, lock, requester, or address-range filtering exists inside fuse_mem. Top-level connects pkt_wrapper fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata to fuse_mem and also routes jtag_hash_o/ikey_hash_o/okey_hash_o into dmi_jtag.
evidence_slots.path: NoC pkt port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req/fuse_addr -> fuse_mem.rdata_o; fuse_mem.jtag_hash_o -> riscv_peripherals.jtag_hash -> dmi_jtag.jtag_hash_i
evidence_slots.state_condition: fuse_mem has constant contents and no reset-controlled clear. Any asserted req_i captures the address. rdata_o reflects mem[addr_q] for addr_q < MEM_SIZE. jtag_hash_o/okey_hash_o/ikey_hash_o are continuously derived from mem entries.
evidence_slots.impact: If pkt_wrapper exposes fuse_req/fuse_addr to MMIO/NoC requesters without adequate filtering, attackers can read debug authentication hash and other keys, undermining debug authentication and crypto secrecy.

rtl_evidence.detail: mem contains JTAG expected HMAC hash, HMAC hashes/key and crypto keys; req_i captures addr_q; rdata_o directly returns mem[addr_q]; hash outputs are constructed directly from mem entries.

declares fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata; pkt_wrapper drives fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o and consumes fuse_rdata_i; fuse_mem connects jtag_hash/ikey_hash/okey_hash and read interface; dmi_jtag receives those hashes.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: fuse_mem exposes security-sensitive fuse contents, including JTAG authentication hash and other key material, through a generic rdata_o path without internal privilege, lock, or address filtering.
inspection_records.notes: pkt_wrapper internals are not provided, so the candidate hinges on exposure through that wrapper; fuse_mem itself lacks internal protection.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible register interface / NoC requester

evidence_slots.expected_guard: Writes to acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl should require authorized privilege and sticky lock enforcement; once locked, protected configuration/peripheral writes should be blocked.

The actual write update logic for reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl and sticky lock behavior resides in wrapper modules not included in the provided evidence.

evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset defaults and sticky set/clear behavior of reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl are not visible. Top-level reset passes rst_ni into wrappers; policy state is output from wrappers.
evidence_slots.impact: If wrappers do not enforce sticky locks and privilege checks, software/NoC requesters may rewrite policy or disable protections; visible top-level shows intended policy distribution but not enough evidence for a violation.

rtl_evidence.detail: declares reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, acc_ctrl_c; acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]).
search results show reglk_ctrl slices and acc_ctrl_c are passed into many peripheral wrappers; reglk_wrapper outputs reglk_ctrl.

evidence_slots.subject: test/JTAG requester using testmode_i and TAP pins
evidence_slots.operation: shift/capture/update DMI data or select DMI chain in test mode
evidence_slots.object: DMI scan chain / TDO output / debug request path
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Test mode or TAP operation should not enable unauthorized DMI access or expose DMI response data on TDO unless normal debug authorization/unlock and TAP state checks are satisfied.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag_tap selects tdo_mux = dmi_tdo_i when jtag_ir_q is DMIACCESS and drives tdo_oe_o when shift_ir or shift_dr_o. dmi_access_o is an IR-selection output from TAP logic, with no evidence of jtag_unlock/pass_check gating in the TAP. testmode_i only selects between inverted and non-inverted TCK via a DFT clock mux, but the DMIACCESS TDO path and output enable are not auth-gated.
evidence_slots.path: JTAG TAP testmode_i/DFT clock mux -> TAP state shift_dr_o -> DMIACCESS tdo_mux(dmi_tdo_i) -> td_o/tdo_oe_o; DMIACCESS/update_dr -> dmi_jtag request FSM
evidence_slots.state_condition: When trst_ni is low, td_o and tdo_oe_o reset to 0. Otherwise, during shift_dr_o, tdo_oe_o is asserted and DMIACCESS shifts dmi_tdo_i. testmode_i asserted changes tck_n selection only, not authorization.
evidence_slots.impact: An unauthenticated JTAG/test requester can select DMIACCESS and observe DMI scan-chain bits/TDO activity; combined with the DMI read gating issue, this can expose debug response data while locked.

rtl_evidence.detail: tdo_mux selects dmi_tdo_i for DMIACCESS.
testmode_i only selects DFT TCK mux clock input.
td_o <= tdo_mux and tdo_oe_o <= shift_ir | shift_dr_o, with no auth guard.
TAP FSM asserts capture/shift/update DR based on TAP state/TMS.
dmi_jtag_tap outputs dmi_access, shift/update/capture and TDO signals directly into dmi_jtag.

inspection_records.candidate_finding: TAP DMI scan selection and TDO output are not gated by debug authentication/unlock; testmode changes the TCK clock mux but normal shifting of DMI response data to TDO is available whenever the TAP selects DMIACCESS and enters shift states.
inspection_records.notes: The violation is primarily lack of auth gating around DMI scan/output; testmode_i itself appears limited to DFT clock selection in the provided snippet.

evidence_slots.subject: external JTAG authentication requester
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Unlock should be granted only after HMAC computation is complete and digest equals provisioned jtag_hash_i; bypass modes and pass_mode should not allow unconditional or stale unlock.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: dmi_jtag contains HMAC-related signals and instance. Provided observation indicates jtag_unlock_o is assigned pass_check; pass_check is defaulted low in always_comb and only asserted transiently in PassChkValid after hashValid/pass_hash comparison. The request FSM allows READ when pass_check is true or we_flag is false. HMAC exposes hash_valid_o/ready_o but persistence and stale digest clearing are only partly visible.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_jtag pass_data/startHash/pass_mode -> hmac instance -> pass_hash/hashValid/hmac_ready -> pass_check -> jtag_unlock_o and DMI request FSM
evidence_slots.state_condition: dmi_jtag TAP reset clears FSM and registers; pass_check/pass_mode/startHash are combinational defaults. HMAC reset clears state/hash_o/ready_o/hash_valid_o. hash_valid_o is cleared on init_i in hmac IDLE; hmac retains hash_o until reset or next computation.
evidence_slots.impact: Authentication state may be transient and not a robust session lock; more critically, reads may bypass HMAC when we_flag is deasserted. This weakens the intended JTAG password/HMAC protection.

rtl_evidence.detail: authentication states/signals: PassChk/PassChkWait/PassChkValid, pass_check, pass_mode, startHash, exp_hash/pass_hash, pass_data, hmac_ready, hashValid.

provided observation: jtag_unlock_o assigned pass_check; pass_check default low; READ can depend on ~we_flag; pass-check valid state sets pass_check based on HMAC comparison; hmac instance consumes hashes/pass_data.
HMAC reset initializes state/hash outputs; init_i clears hash_valid_o and starts computation; key_hash_bypass_i changes SHA init/hash-block update behavior.
top connects we_flag_4 and fuse-derived jtag_hash/okey_hash/ikey_hash to dmi_jtag and receives jtag_unlock.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Full dmi_jtag lines 90-199 and 318-334 were partly truncated; exact comparison expression and pass_data loading are from provided model observation rather than full raw snippet.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: The unlock output is tied to a transient combinational pass_check rather than a persistent authenticated state, and DMI reads can be allowed by ~we_flag without pass_check; the observed design does not ensure all debug access follows HMAC-valid comparison.

The visible AXI-lite bridge wiring for dm_master exists, but return/grant signals are tied off and no alternate fabric bridge is shown in the provided evidence.
evidence_slots.path: dm_top master signals -> dm_master AXI-lite bridge/wires -> tied-off grant/response in riscv_peripherals
evidence_slots.state_condition: Tie-offs are continuous assignments independent of reset or authorization state.
evidence_slots.impact: Within the visible build, debug master cannot complete system bus reads/writes through this path; no permission violation is supported for master range bypass.

rtl_evidence.detail: comment says system bus accesses not supported; dm_master_gnt, dm_master_r_valid, dm_master_r_rdata assigned '0.
unused AXI-lite master metadata fields tied to zero.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Conditional build alternatives outside the provided file/scope were not shown; conclusion applies to visible RTL observations.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible reset interface and/or JTAG debug interface
evidence_slots.object: reset control signals rst_1..rst_13, ndmreset_o, dmi_rst_no and security state affected by reset
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Reset/lifecycle operations affecting harts/peripherals/debug should require privilege/access-control or debug authorization, and reset should clear sensitive auth/session state safely without clearing sticky protections unexpectedly.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level declares many rst_* signals and passes rst_i into wrappers. dmi_jtag assigns dmi_rst_no = rst_ni, and top-level leaves dmi_rst_no unconnected. dmi_jtag_tap includes DTMCS dmireset/dmihardreset fields and dmi_reset_o used to clear sticky DMI error state. ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/debug_req_o are outputs from dm_top, but their authorization semantics are inside dm_top, not provided. Reset-control wrapper decode for rst_* is not present in the visible evidence.
evidence_slots.path: reset NoC/MMIO path and debug DMI reset fields -> wrapper rst_* outputs and dm_top ndmreset/debug controls; dmi_jtag DTMCS reset -> dmi_reset error clear
evidence_slots.state_condition: TAP trst_ni reset clears TAP/DMI shift state. HMAC reset clears HMAC state/hash valid. dmi_reset from TAP clears DMI error when dtmcs_select. Effects on reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl and rst_* generation are not visible.
evidence_slots.impact: Unauthorized reset/lifecycle manipulation cannot be confirmed or dismissed; critical decode and security-state reset behavior resides in unprovided modules.

rtl_evidence.detail: declares rst_1..rst_11 and rst_13 reset-control signals.
dmi_rst_no is rst_ni; dmi_reset && dtmcs_select clears error; trst_ni resets DMI shift/FSM state.
DTMCS has dmihardreset/dmireset fields; TAP FSM generates dmi_reset_o/update states.
dmi_rst_no from dmi_jtag is not connected; top-level exposes ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, debug_req_o.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need reset wrapper/decode and dm_top behavior to verify guards for rst_* and ndmreset_o.

evidence_slots.subject: DMA engine or software configuring DMA via NoC/MMIO
evidence_slots.operation: configure DMA or perform DMA read/write transfer
evidence_slots.object: DMA registers/transactions and protected memory/peripheral regions
evidence_slots.expected_guard: DMA configuration and active transfers should be restricted by privilege/access-control and range/PMP policy at configuration and use time.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level instantiates dma_wrapper with reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][8]), pmpcfg_i/pmpaddr_i, we_flag_3, and AXI req/resp from a DMA NoC AXI-lite bridge. This indicates intended controls are supplied. The actual DMA configuration registers, transfer-time checks, target address filtering, and race behavior are in dma_wrapper, not provided.
evidence_slots.path: buf_ariane_dma_noc2 -> noc_axilite_bridge -> dma_axi_req/resp -> dma_wrapper with acc_ctrl/reglk/PMP inputs
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset defaults and active-transfer policy update behavior are not visible. AXI metadata is tied off after wrapper/bridge wiring but does not show access decisions.
evidence_slots.impact: No direct top-level bypass is proven, but DMA access-control correctness cannot be established without dma_wrapper internals.

rtl_evidence.detail: dma_wrapper instance receives reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, pmpcfg_i, pmpaddr_i, we_flag, AXI req/resp, rst_8.
DMA NoC AXI-lite bridge connects buf_ariane_dma_noc2/noc3 ports to dma_axi_req/resp.
DMA AXI request metadata fields are tied off; no range policy visible there.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need dma_wrapper internals to inspect config-time and transfer-time access checks and target ranges.

evidence_slots.subject: external observer through JTAG/TDO or software-visible interface
evidence_slots.object: HMAC key/input-derived internal state and hash output used for authentication
evidence_slots.expected_guard: HMAC key material/internal state should not be directly exposed on TDO/MMIO; hash_o should only feed intended comparison/authentication unless authorized.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: hmac module computes ipad/opad from key_i and produces hash_o/ready/hash_valid. dmi_jtag instantiates hmac for authentication. Provided evidence does not show hash_o connected to top-level MMIO/TDO; dmi_jtag TDO path shifts DMI register data, not directly hmac hash_o in the visible snippets. However full dmi_jtag dataflow around pass_hash/pass_data and any readback of authentication registers was truncated, so exposure cannot be fully ruled out.
evidence_slots.path: dmi_jtag HMAC instance key/hash inputs from fuse_mem -> hmac hash_o/pass_hash -> dmi_jtag comparison/control; possible but unproven path to DMI scan data/TDO
evidence_slots.state_condition: On HMAC reset, state/hash_o/ready/hash_valid are cleared. On init_i, hash_valid_o is cleared and computation starts. key_hash_bypass_i affects SHA initialization/hash-block update. hash_o retention until next computation/reset is visible but consumers are not fully visible.
evidence_slots.impact: No direct secret-output exposure is proven from provided snippets; stale digest/bypass implications require full dmi_jtag consumer inspection.

rtl_evidence.detail: HMAC key/message/hash ports; ipad/opad derived from key; reset clears hash_o/hash_valid; key_hash_bypass_i controls SHA init vs hash-block update.
dmi_jtag has exp_hash/pass_hash/pass_data/hmac_ready/hashValid and instantiates hmac (full connection details partly truncated).
TDO can shift dmi_tdo_i for DMIACCESS, but direct HMAC hash_o routing to dmi_tdo_i is not shown.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Need complete dmi_jtag HMAC instance connections and DMI register/shift data assignments to determine whether pass_hash/hash_o can be read through DMI/TDO.
